q: Plants lack a nervous system like animals. (2026)
Internal stimuli or internal control are chemicals called plant hormones. (2025)
Plants can respond to their environment in various ways. (2024)
Growth in general is considered as an increase in size of the object. (2025)
Meristematic phase occurs in shoot apex and root apex. (2025)
Growth can be defined as an irreversible increase in size of an individual cell or organ or its parts. (2025)
Growth regulators are manufactured by dead protoplasm. (2024)
Genetic factors are not involved in overall maintenance of a plant. (2024)
All parts of seed plants produce cytokinin. (2026)
Auxins are strong organic acids produced naturally by plants. (2025)
Auxin, gibberellin and cytokinin are growth inhibitors for plants. (2025)
A plant hormone is an inorganic compound. (2024)
In hypogeal germination, the cotyledons are first pushed above the ground by the rapid elongation of hypocotyl. (2026)
Hypogeal germination takes place above the ground. (2024)
Soybean is short night plant. (2025)
Effect or requirements of relative length of day and night on flowering is called photoperiodism. (2024 / 2025)
A stimulus is detected by a sensory receptor. (2025)
Reflex arcs are simple connections of only two neurones to transmit messages. (2025)
Motorneurones are found entirely within the central nervous system. (2026)
Neurones are the basic structural and functional units of the nervous system. (2025)
Individual neurones are organized into tissues called glial cells. (2025)
The endocrine glands and their hormones make up the nervous system. (2025)
Endocrine glands secrete chemical messengers called hormones. (2025)
